description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a crustacean, likely a Norway lobster or similar species, with a slender, elongated body and a segmented exoskeleton. The body is divided into a robust cephalothorax and a long, tapering abdomen, both covered in a hard, reddish-orange shell with lighter and darker mottling. Prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, flanked by multiple walking legs, and two long, thin antennae project forward from the head. The eyes are set on short stalks, providing a wide field of vision. The tail fans out at the end, displaying distinct segmentation and lateral extensions. The overall appearance is lifelike, with fine details in the limbs, joints, and surface texture.
description: A reddish-orange segmented crustacean with long antennae, prominent claws, and a fan-shaped tail. Its body is detailed with a hard shell and multiple articulated legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, claws, antennae, and the segmented tail extending backward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two long antennae, forward-facing claws, and the symmetry of the cephalothorax.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the legs, claws, and the elongated abdomen.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the fan-shaped tail, segmentation of the abdomen, and arrangement of the walking legs.
